roleLine: Computer Science student focused on building clean, useful software.

paragraphs: Hello, my name is Kevin Trinh. I'm currently an undergraduate student at the University of Houston pursuing a B.S. in Computer Science. Recently, I built a full-stack app used by 100+ students that helps UH students understand their degree progress and get schedule recommendations.

I'm interested in machine learning, operating systems, and full-stack development, and I care a lot about clean UI and maintainable architecture. Outside of tech, I’m really into desk setups and PCs, and I spend my free time playing chess, thrifting, and gaming.

hero.headline: I build modern full stack apps and free tools that save time.

paragraphs: I’m a full-stack focused, performance-first builder who’s obsessed with clean UI and maintainable architecture. I’m currently an undergraduate student at the University of Houston pursuing a B.S. in Computer Science, and I’m almost always building something on the side. I like creating products that feel fast, clean, and genuinely useful — especially tools that remove friction, reduce busywork, and make people’s day easier.

A lot of my work is full-stack end to end: modern UI, a reliable backend, and practical features that actually get used. Recently, I built a full-stack app used by 100+ students that helps UH students understand degree progress and get schedule recommendations. I care a lot about polish — spacing, clarity, and performance — and I’m constantly improving my engineering habits by focusing on clean architecture, better reliability, and systems that scale without turning into spaghetti.

now.title: What I’m currently working on
paragraphs: Right now I’m iterating on DevfolioX (a clean portfolio template for developers) and expanding CoogPlanner — a full-stack app that helps UH students understand degree progress and build better schedules.

I also recently started VisibleSeed — where I build fast, modern websites and automation systems for local businesses. My focus is shipping reliable features, keeping the UI crisp, and making everything easy to use.

usage.title: Usage
usage.description: The stack and tools I like using day to day.

setup.title: Setup
paragraphs: I’m big on a clean, fast setup that keeps me in flow — minimal friction, quick feedback loops, and tools that don’t fight you.
My ideal setup is simple: fast machine, tidy workspace, and a workflow that makes it easy to stay consistent.
setup.caption: Clean workspace, fast feedback loops, and minimal friction.
